jobs.description:
We invent bold solutions in sustainable transportation and returns experiences which create trust and value for customers, selling partners, and shippers that benefit people, planet, and profit. We champion and lead the way to achieve Zero Defects, Zero Cost of Returns, Zero Product Waste and Zero Carbon, at scale.

Worldwide Returns & ReCommerce enhances the relationship with Amazon’s customers through Support Options and a Returns process that is simple, transparent, and convenient. We work to reduce returns by helping customers keep what they bought and removing defects that would cause a return (Zero Defects). When items are returned or damaged, we maximize reuse and recovery to achieve Zero Cost of Returns and Zero Product Waste, benefiting people, planet, and profit.

The WWR&R Finance team seeks a self-driven Finance Manager excited about solving complex problems that have a customer facing impact, require strategic long-term thinking and help drive the 4 zeroes.

As the Finance Manager, you will partner directly with business, tech and finance teams to identify the right business strategies, help define roadmaps, and provide guidance and reporting. You will play a critical role in communicating business strategy and results with company leadership up to the SVP level. You will own a product that supports a Senior Executive Team member's goal, and help build a robust product that is error-free, can scale and is reliable. As a key member of the team, you will influence business model, long term vision and decisions that will substantially reduce cost to sere customers. You will have a strong operational and controllership mindset as we continue our mission to build best-in-class reverse logistics processes.

The successful candidate has demonstrated the following abilities: they influence business owners, support thoughtful decision-making, and adapt to a rapidly-evolving environment. They collaborate with senior executives and various business units across Amazon. They bring excellent verbal and written communication skills. A successful candidate is an effective multitasker, adept at identifying high-priority work and projects that maximize results for key stakeholders, and excited about solving complex problems that affect company-wide strategic decisions.



Key job responsibilities
- Own definitions, automation roadmap and data integrity for the NCRC P&L, which is used to plan and report for an S-Team goal
- Use solid financial knowledge and business acumen to build financial models, interpret data, draw conclusions, and make recommendations that deliver results, drive improvements, and influence leaders throughout Amazon. These decisions can have global impact.
- Uses SQL, financial systems and advanced MS Excel functions to work with large data sets. Outputs plans, forecasts, budgets, and consolidations.
- Manages roadmap for product and prioritizes feature development based on impact
- Creates effective business partnerships, manages competing priorities, and provides guidance based on data analysis during unexpected events.
- Uses active listening skills intuitively to communicate data (both written and verbal) in a clear, concise, and unambiguous manner. Adjusts style for various audiences to articulate complex finance issues clearly.
- Collects and analyzes quantitative data to develop relevant reports that drive cost reductions.
- Participates in hiring, developing, and mentoring peers and team members. Seeks and leverages diverse perspectives in all activities.
- Identifies opportunities to resolve defects proactively. Improves, scales, or simplifies finance tools across teams and organizations.
- Contributes to work life harmony by being a positive, collaborative, and effective influencer to new team members as an indicator of operations excellence.


About the team
The WWRR finance team works on high visibility, complex deliverables that have a huge impact on Amazon's bottom-line. We partner with senior stakeholders across WWRR as well as partner teams. It will be a challenging role with lots of opportunities to learn and grow. You will be part of a close-knit team that supports each other and learns together.

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

We are currently seeking a Data Center Project Manager to serve as a technical resource within Amazon data centers. You will be part of a highly creative, efficient team tasked with tackling the most fascinating and challenges in designing, building, and operating Amazon data facilities. The Project Manager is ultimately responsible for project oversight and review of all disciplines including electrical, mechanical, controls, and architectural. A fundamental understanding of these systems is required, as the individual will be expected to identify areas for improvement as well as act on data provided from other team and organizations. Ideal candidates will possess the ability to design, develop and deploy innovative solutions to address operational challenges. This role requires both independent contribution as well as the ability to work within multi-disciplinary teams. The scope of projects will include but not limited to the improvement of electrical, mechanical, fire detection and building automation system. The Project Manager is responsible for the overall direction, coordination, implementation, execution, control and completion of specific projects ensuring consistency with company strategy, commitments and goals.

If you are passionate about the Customer Experience; you think big; and you want to contribute to the operational excellence of Amazon data centers then this may be the challenge you’ve been looking for!

About the team
AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS)
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.
You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

*Why AWS*
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

*Diverse Experiences*
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

*Work/Life Balance*
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

*Inclusive Team Culture*
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

*Mentorship and Career Growth*
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
Transform network infrastructure by joining our dynamic team! You'll be at the forefront of developing innovative solutions that drive network stability and scalability, using your technical expertise to create automated systems that power global technological advancement.

Network Install Technicians will collaborate with cross-functional teams, troubleshoot complex technical scenarios, and implement strategic solutions that keep our network running seamlessly. Expect to engage in hands-on technical work that requires creativity, precision, and a passion for technological innovation.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.

NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.


Key job responsibilities
• Moving and implementing large enterprise equipment
• Installing Fiber and Copper cabling and patching cabling between equipment
• Ensuring proper safety and security standards are maintained during cabling and rack installation,
• Following well established hardware installation procedures and break-fix activities
• Safely using material handling tools and equipment
• Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required. You will be responsible for having a reliable personal vehicle and valid driver's license to travel within the regional work area, as company transportation will not be provided


Physical Requirements:
• Regularly lift and/or move up to 40 pounds; and participate in group lifts for 41+ pounds
• Ability to work in an environment that operates 24/7 with an ability to participate in primary responder rotation and provide after-hours support as needed
• Working in cramped and/or elevated and/or noisy environments
• Bending, lifting, stretching, reaching, standing and walking for up to 8+ hours a day
• Ascending and descending ladders, stairs, and gangways safely and without limitation

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:
• Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed
• Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational
• Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility
Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.

jobs.title: Network Install Technician

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of technical program management experience
- Experience using one or more of the following CAD technologies: AutoCAD, Rhino, Revit, SolidWorks, CREO, BIM360, SketchUp or KeyShot
- Bachelor's degree in Telecommunications, Mechanical, or Structural Engineering, or related discipline, or equivalent work experience
- 4+ years of experience in network pathway infrastructure design or installation in data center environments
- Experience managing quality across the full project lifecycle, from design review through installation validation and defect remediation
- Proficiency of NEMA VE1/VE2 or IEC 61537 for cable tray construction and installation
jobs.description:
Work with construction teams to integrate quality assurance into data center network pathway infrastructure construction throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in construction design reviews, page turns, and reviews for construction documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during construction. You'll influence construction managers, general contractors, Colocation providers and relevant contractors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical construction milestone checks for pathway infrastructure.

You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from construction teams and internal stakeholders to refine quality processes. You'll verify that pathway infrastructure construction meets installation requirements by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections. You'll document and align on construction standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and construction methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with construction managers, and provide guidelines to general contractors, Colocation providers and contractors to ensure consistent execution across all construction activities.

This role matters because construction quality directly impacts data center reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in construction design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on.

Key job responsibilities
- Own Quality Assurance related programs to drive world-class quality in network infrastructure in the new construction of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Infra Quality Program Manager - Construction will be obsessed with quality, engineering, and design continuous improvements.

- Review cable tray designs against Amazon's Policies and Industry Standards and define acceptance checklists for each build milestone.

- Partner with Construction Management team and Technical Infrastructure Project Manager to ensure network pathway infrastructures are built according to design documents and build project milestones, document early signals for risks and escalate to respective owners for remediations.

- Influence construction partners and stakeholders through quality mechanisms and standards to drive consistent high-quality deliverables across all network pathway infrastructure projects. Establish frameworks for cross-functional collaboration that result in sustained quality improvements.

- Capture requirement variants between approved design and physical installation and collaborate with onsite teams and design teams to close gaps in pathway infrastructure installation methods.

- Define and maintain Quality Assurance documents including inspection checklists and installation requirements from design specifications, Quality Assurance standard operating procedures, Global Design Standards documents, Global Installation Best Practice Guidelines, Product Specifications for Inside Plant cable routing, and Quality Issue trackers.

- Monitor installations and collect lessons learned on network pathway quality to drive continuous improvement across the program.

- Drive Network Pathway Infrastructure Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support Construction teams and provide reference guidelines for vendors.

- Define quality and operational metrics related to construction performance, establish measurement frameworks, and analyze data to drive strategic improvements and inform decision-making.

A day in the life
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites.

As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned.

About the team
AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain.

You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers.

- Experience conducting quality inspections in live data center environments with engagement of contractors, engineers, and stakeholders
- Certification in quality management disciplines (e.g., ASQ CQE, CQA, or equivalent)
- BICSI RCDD Certification or internationally recognized equivalent (e.g. CDCDP, DCDC, etc.)
- Possess excellent communication skills, problem solving, attention to detail, root cause analysis experience, and ability to maintain high quality standards
- Proven track record on ability to identify creative and innovative solutions to improve quality, reliability, or maintainability (Removed for AMER)

jobs.title: Network Infrastructure Construction Quality Program Manager , Global Quality Team

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience presenting root cause analysis and error correction from operational events to senior management
- Experience with CAD software (AutoCAD, REVIT, BIM 360, etc.)
- Bachelor's degree in Telecommunications, Mechanical, or Structural Engineering, or related discipline, or equivalent work experience
- 4+ years of experience in network pathway infrastructure design or installation in data center environments
- Proficiency of NEMA VE1/VE2 or IEC 61537 for cable tray construction and installation.
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain.

You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers.

In this role, you will:

Work with our data center design engineering teams, network new product team, and construction teams to integrate quality assurance throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in design reviews, page turns, and Building Information Modeling reviews for Basis of Design documents and construction drawing sets, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met from concept through construction. You'll influence design teams, construction partners, and other stakeholders to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to Amazon, Industry, and jurisdictional standards during critical milestone checks for design, construction, and cabling activities.

You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from internal stakeholders and refining quality processes based on their needs. You'll verify that designs and construction meet standards by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections. You'll document and align design standards and standard operating procedures as new technology integrates into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with partner teams to ensure consistent execution across all projects.

This role matters because design and construction quality directly impacts data center reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in early design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on.

Key job responsibilities
Own Quality Assurance related programs to drive world-class quality in network infrastructure design of new Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Infra Quality Engineer - Design will be obsessed with quality, engineering, and design continuous improvements

Partner with the Information Communication Technology Design team to ensure network pathway infrastructure designs align with Data Center Construction build plans

Define and maintain updates of Quality Assurance documents including inspection checklists and installation requirements from design specifications, Global Design Standards documents, Global Installation Best Practice Guidelines, Product Specifications for Inside Plant Cabling, Basis of Design and Quality Issue trackers.

Partner with the Information Communication Technology Design team and New Product Development team to implement review mechanisms for new designs and design updates to identify installation gaps and create standard operating procedures to manage remediation plans

Monitor deployments and collect lessons learned on network pathway quality

Define Network Pathway Infrastructure Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support stakeholders and as reference guidelines for vendors.

Define quality and operational metrics to effectively measure performance and success

Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to perform site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel



This is an opportunity to leverage and grow your existing program management, quality assurance and engineering experience in an innovative and highly collaborative environment where the results of your work will directly impact AWS and its customers.


A day in the life
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites.

As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned.

jobs.title: Network Infrastructure Design Quality Engineer , Global Cabling Quality

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Knowledge of network cabling, optic types, and test equipment
- Associate's degree in Telecommunications or related discipline, or equivalent work experience
- 2+ years of experience Inside Plant cabling installation in data center environments

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain.

You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers.

In this role, you will:

Work with Network Deployment and Vendor Management teams to integrate quality assurance into cable installation processes throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in reviews of training curriculums and cabling design documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during cable installation activities. You'll influence partner teams and cabling vendors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical milestone checks for cable installation and testing activities.

You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from internal stakeholders and cabling vendors, then refining quality processes based on their needs. You'll verify that cable installations meet standards by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections and cable validation testing. You'll drive cable validation testing adoption and defect management while establishing performance benchmarks to improve vendor performance and enhance quality metrics. You'll document and align cable installation standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and cabling methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with partner teams and provide guidelines to cabling vendors to ensure consistent execution across all network installations.

This role matters because cable installation quality directly impacts data center network reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in cabling design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on.

Key job responsibilities
Support Quality Assurance related programs to drive world-class quality in network cabling in the new constructions of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Cabling Quality Technician will be obsessed with quality, engineering and design continuous improvements

Partner with Network Deployment teams to adopt defined quality assurance checklists, gather feedback, and generate recommendations to improve checklists. Work with partner teams to track cabling scope changes and perform impact analysis to address business needs

Partner with the Vendor Management team to drive cable vendor quality, provide vendor performance metrics through business reviews, and refine processes to improve vendor performance

Support cable installation benchmark according to Amazon policies and industry standards; maintain cable installation standards, checklists, standard operating procedures and best practices guidelines

Conduct Network Cabling Installation Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support internal and external partner and stakeholder teams

Collaborate with stakeholder teams to capture metrics data such as inspection reports and cable test results; track defects for root cause analysis, generate recommendations, evaluate vendor performances and escalate issues to respective owners for remediation plans

Track and report on project milestones, vendor performance metrics, and quality KPIs including First Pass Yield, defect rates, and schedule adherence across multiple concurrent projects

Support continuous improvement initiatives by capturing lessons learned, facilitating post-project reviews, and implementing process improvements to enhance future project delivery

Identify opportunities to reduce Cost of Poor Quality and improve overall project efficiency

Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to attend site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel



This is an opportunity to leverage and grow your existing program management, quality assurance and engineering experience in an innovative and highly collaborative environment where the results of your work will directly impact AWS and its customers.

A day in the life
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites.

As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned.

jobs.company_name: Amazon Data Services MX, S. de
jobs.description:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is growing rapidly, and we are looking for a Data Center Technician to join our expanding Infrastructure Operations team.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a fast paced technology company and a leader in the world of data centers. The position will help ensure overall availability and reliability to meet or exceed defined service levels of data center operations. This position involves hardware and network diagnostics followed by physical repair and may include participation in an on-call rotation. All candidates should be willing to work both independently and with a team. Work prioritization, organizational skills, effective communication, and the ability to react quickly are critical to being successful. In addition to hardware and network repair candidates will install equipment, create documentation, innovate solutions, and fix problems within the Data Center Space. This team works in an environment that operates 24/7.

Key job responsibilities
• Experience working in a Data Center environment
• Understanding of network design, protocols and troubleshooting.
• Understanding of storage devices.
• Experience in Hardware diagnostics and repair.
• Software installation and configuration.
• Experience managing work and priorities through a ticketing system
• rotational Shiftwork


About the team
Diverse Experiences

Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of B2B sales experience
- Experience using sales CRM tools such as Salesforce or similar software
- Experience in account management and selling of digital media
jobs.description:
Amazon Advertising operates at the intersection of eCommerce and advertising, offering a rich array of digital display advertising solutions with the goal of helping our customers find and discover anything they want to buy. We help brands reach Amazon customers through strategic, highly targeted self-service advertising products. If you’re interested in joining a rapidly growing team working to build a unique, world-class advertising group with a relentless focus on the customer, you’ve come to the right place. This is an opportunity to sell world class personalization technologies and drive sales across multiple platforms to new and existing small-to-medium brand and performance advertisers. There are also opportunities to grow and retain revenue from existing advertisers. If you have a consultative selling style and are able to tactfully guide customers to their goals by optimizing for the long-term, apply today!
Key job responsibilities
• Use your influencing and relationship-building skills to prospect, penetrate and develop executive-level relationships with clients, uncovering the business needs of Amazon's clients
• Retain and grow revenue from existing advertisers
• Drive deals to closure in a new business environment
• You influence how decisions are made by leading with data, and are focused on understanding how Amazon's products and tools can help build relevant advertising solutions for our customers
• Deliver the highest level of sales and customer service to our clients
• Utilize sales CRM to measure progress against pertinent sales activities and opportunities
• Understand and learn about the e-commerce industry and competitive environment, including competitor product offerings
• Collaborate cross-functionally to create and execute advertising plans for different types of businesses


Key job responsibilities
Revenue Generation & Business Development
• Proven track record of exceeding revenue targets, identifying new business opportunities, and building strategic growth plans that drive significant YoY revenue expansion across partner portfolio and new business acquisition
Strategic Client Leadership
• Excellence in C-level relationship building and account penetration, with demonstrated success in managing multi-million dollar partnerships and securing executive buy-in for strategic investments
Product Expertise
• Strong consultative selling approach backed by deep knowledge of Amazon's advertising solutions, competitive landscape, and ability to translate complex product offerings into compelling business value propositions
Deal Management & Execution
• Track record of leading high-value, complex deal cycles from prospecting to close, while orchestrating cross-functional resources to drive partner adoption and revenue acceleration
Data-Driven Sales Strategy
• Exceptional ability to leverage data and analytics to build compelling business cases, identify upsell opportunities, and create strategic account plans that drive sustained revenue growth
Client Success & Growth
• History of expanding client relationships through account management, proactive opportunity identification, and proven ability to position solutions that address critical business challenges


About the team
Being successful on this team requires the ability to:
-Drive advertising revenue within Sponsored Ads and DVA, Video solutions
-Ability to identify revenue trends through data and create actionable steps to optimize growth
-Understand, adopt, evangelize, and communicate the Amazon Advertising product suite and capabilities
-Work across internal and external teams to execute campaigns; successfully navigate and leverage internal tools and resources from pre- to post-sale
-Host and facilitate monthly/quarterly business review with internal stakeholders/Partners/Clients
-Understand retail dynamics and how Amazon Advertising solutions affects long term growth for advertiser partners
-Understand digital advertising and retail metrics, in order to direct next steps with advertising partner accounts.
